model: Five-tier progressive track model (T0–T4) — per-concept difficulty assignment
note: Each concept is assigned to a track by its own difficulty field. A domain may contribute concepts to multiple tracks (e.g., math.nt spans T1–T4). Learner progression: T0 → T1 → T2 → T3 → T4.

tracks.name: Absolute Foundations
tracks.description: Entry-level mathematics. No prerequisites assumed. Covers logic, sets, number sense, and basic arithmetic.

tracks.name: Pre-University / Early Undergraduate
tracks.description: Pre-university to first-year university. Algebra, geometry, trigonometry, functions, sequences, introductory number theory.

tracks.name: Undergraduate Core
tracks.description: Standard second/third-year university. Calculus, differential equations, linear algebra, probability, statistics.

tracks.name: Upper Undergraduate / Early Graduate
tracks.description: Third/fourth-year and early MSc. Abstract algebra, real/complex analysis, topology, numerical methods, optimization.

tracks.name: Graduate / Research Mathematics
tracks.description: MSc/PhD level. Measure theory, functional analysis, category theory, research-frontier topics.
